title: Lesson Quiz: Limits on Rights — Balancing rights, public safety, and order.
description: Assessment of the concept that Balancing rights, public safety, and order.

questions.prompt: Why can the government place limits on rights in some situations?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rights are protected, not eliminated.

choices.text: To protect public safety, order, and the rights of others
choices.feedback: Correct. Rights can be limited when necessary to protect society and others.
Incorrect. Any limits must still follow constitutional rules.

choices.text: To give one group more rights than another
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Limits are not meant to create unfair treatment.

questions.prompt: Rights in the Constitution are always unlimited.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rights are protected, but they are not unlimited.
Correct. Rights can be limited in certain situations.

questions.prompt: Which situation best shows a lawful limit on a right?

choices.text: A protest must follow permit and safety rules
choices.feedback: Correct. Safety rules can be used to balance rights with public order.

choices.text: A person loses all rights permanently for expressing an opinion
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That would be an unfair and excessive restriction.

choices.text: A government official ignores the Constitution
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Limits must still follow constitutional law.

choices.text: A law is applied randomly with no reason
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Limits must be based on lawful reasons, not random action.

questions.prompt: Courts may help decide whether a limit on a right is reasonable.

choices.feedback: Correct. Courts often review whether limits on rights are lawful and reasonable.
Incorrect. Courts do help decide whether limits are allowed.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea behind limits on rights?

choices.text: Rights should only apply to certain groups
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rights are broadly protected, not limited to certain groups.
Incorrect. Rights are protected, but their limits may be evaluated in real situations.

choices.text: The government should always win conflicts over rights
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Limits must be justified and balanced.

choices.text: Rights must be balanced with safety, order, and the rights of others
choices.feedback: Correct. The central idea is balance between individual freedom and the needs of society.
